spans.text: the poor passengers of life's rude bourn
a budding spray , And like a sea-maid sung her sweetly warbled lay
The grisly phantoms of the night
their spectre-terrors the green sea That whispers at my feet , the matin gale That crisps its shining marge shall solace thee
my feet , the matin gale That crisps its shining marge shall solace thee
The soothest sprite that sings on life's long wilderness
As slowly ceased her tender voice
As slowly ceased her tender voice
over the level seas Spring's healing airs blew soft
the level seas Spring's healing airs
Above whose peaceful umbrage , trailing high , A little smoke
Above whose peaceful umbrage , trailing high , A little smoke
Above whose peaceful umbrage , trailing high , A little smoke
Upon whose top a spiry citadel Lifted its dim-seen turrets to the skies , Where some high lord of the domain might dwell
some high lord of the domain
to the skies , Where some high lord of the domain
The woods , the sea , the hills that shone so fair , Till woods , and sea , and hills
The woods , the sea , the hills that shone so fair , Till woods , and sea , and hills
a various throng To this enchanting spot their footsteps bend
a harebell garland on his head
I : Mistress of the magic song , O , pity 'twere that hearts that know no guile Should ever feel the pangs of truth or wrong

'twere that hearts that know no guile Should ever feel the pangs of truth or wrong
no guile Should ever feel the pangs of truth or wrong
lovelier smile : Enjoy , OH youth , the season of thy May
He struck at times a small and silver lyre , Or gazed upon the rolling element
his mirror , which did show The various landscape lovelier than the life
The various landscape lovelier than the life
That peeped from her moist lip
to inspire Tumultuous wishes warm , and dreams of fond desire
That still were turned towards the tranquil scene
art thou found Without annoyance in this earthly state
Without annoyance in this earthly state
Come to the high woods and the hills with me , And cast thy sullen myrtle-wreath away
Where a tall vessel rode with sail unfurled , And soon he thought to reach the farther land , Which to his eager eye seemed like a world That he by strength might win and make his own
to reach the farther land , Which to his eager eye seemed like a world That he by strength might win and make his own
the farther land , Which to his eager eye seemed like a world That he by strength might win and make his own
Of ancient Nile to India's farthest plain
dost build up a name by cruel deeds
more sweet the living landscape bloom
her long-lost friends shall bless her sight
the huge forest's unrejoicing shade Bewildered
a grisly fiend her heart dismayed
lapped in dreams of bliss her slow-consenting soul
A slender garland she of straw had made , Of flowers and rushes from the running brook
dropped her half-made garland on the ground
OH God , be merciful to that poor hapless maid
The whelming main — The wintery wave rolls over his cold head
The whelming main — The wintery wave rolls over his cold head
I never shall behold his form again
His senses pierced by the unwonted tone
the trees that waved , the sun that shone , He cast around an agonised look
with a ghastly smile , that spoke his pain , He hied him to his cave in thickest shades again

I : Master , pleasant is this place , And sweet are those melodious notes I hear , And happy they among man's toiling race Who , of their cares forgetful , wander near ; Me they delight , whom sickness and slow pain Have bowed almost to death with heavy hand
they among man's toiling race Who , of their cares forgetful
they among man's toiling race Who , of their cares forgetful
happy they among man's toiling race Who , of their cares forgetful
whom sickness and slow pain Have bowed
that music bland , Which seems to promise hours of joy to come , And bids me tranquil seek my poor but peaceful home

Alas ! these shadows soon may fly , Like the gay creatures of the element ; Yet do poor mortals still with raptured eye Behold like thee the pictures they present ; And , charmed by Hope's sweet music , on they fare , And think they soon shall reach that blissful goal , Where never more the sullen knell of Care For buried friends and severed loves shall toll : So on they fare , till all their troubles cease , And on a lap of earth they lie them down in peace

the transient shadows of a day
